title: CVA6 Sv39 virtual-address check off-by-one
hook_30s: This is the RISC-V Sv39 spec: virtual-address bits 63 down to 38 must all equal bit 38. This is CVA6's code: it checks bits 63 down to 39. That single off-by-one shipped in a virtual-memory protection check in a production RISC-V core. ChipGPT finds it from the spec in 14 seconds.
category: 25 — spec-to-RTL drift (the #1 root cause of respins)

steps.agent: I'll check this CVA6 Sv39 vaddr canonicality logic against the spec.
Reading the spec; extracting the canonicality rule.
Spec rule: bits 63:38 must equal bit 38 (line 8). RTL uses [63:39] at line 15 — bit 38 excluded.

The overflow (canonicality) reduction at load_store_unit.sv:15 spans vaddr[63:39] instead of the spec-mandated vaddr[63:38]. Bit 38 — the Sv39 sign/canonical bit — is excluded from both the AND-reduction (all-ones) and OR-reduction (all-zeros) tests. As a result, an address whose bit 38 differs from bits[63:39] is judged canonical when it is not.

artifact.explanation: Encodes the spec sentence 'bits 63:38 all equal bit 38, or else a page-fault occurs': page_fault_o must assert exactly when vaddr[63:38] is neither all-ones nor all-zeros. Formal check returned CEX against the buggy [63:39] RTL.
artifact.result: SymbiYosys (z3): counterexample found — bug proven formally

steps.agent: Running the directed test in the simulator.

artifact.name: sv39_canonicality_signbit_included
artifact.stimulus: Drive vaddr and compare page_fault_o to spec model spec_pf = !((&vaddr[63:38])==1 || (|vaddr[63:38])==0). Cases: 0x0000_0040_0000_0000 (bit38=1, higher=0 -> non-canonical), 0x0, 0xFFFF_FFC0_0000_0000 (canonical high), 0xFFFF_FF80_0000_0000 (bit38=0, higher=1 -> non-canonical).
artifact.result_on_buggy: FAIL — dut_pf=0 vs spec_pf=1 for 0x0000_0040_0000_0000 and 0xFFFF_FF80_0000_0000.
artifact.result_on_fixed: PASS — all four vectors match the spec model.

artifact.title: Sv39 vaddr canonicality check off-by-one ([63:39] excludes sign bit 38) — VM protection bypass
artifact.root_cause: The overflow (canonicality) reduction at load_store_unit.sv:15 spans vaddr[63:39] instead of the spec-mandated vaddr[63:38]. Bit 38 — the Sv39 sign/canonical bit — is excluded from both the AND-reduction (all-ones) and OR-reduction (all-zeros) tests. As a result, an address whose bit 38 differs from bits[63:39] is judged canonical when it is not.
artifact.fix: Change the slice from [63:39] to [63:38] so the check includes the sign bit: assign overflow = !((&vaddr[63:38]) == 1'b1 || (|vaddr[63:38]) == 1'b0); (matches [XLEN-1:SV-1] = [63:38] in upstream core).
artifact.impact: Non-canonical virtual addresses (e.g. 0x0000_0040_0000_0000 with bit38=1/high bits=0, or 0xFFFF_FF80_0000_0000 with bit38=0/high bits=1) are not flagged with the address-overflow page fault. They proceed into translation and can alias into mapped regions — a virtual-memory protection bypass and a path for software to depend on reserved high address bits.

artifact.coverage_status: Proven: formal CEX + directed sim FAIL on buggy RTL; both pass on [63:38] fix.
